title: microula bhutanica
intro: Microula bhutanica is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the Himalayas and parts of China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Microula bhutanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
